name: tagic

metadata.name: tagic
metadata.summary: Build html / xhtml with a nice syntax.

documentation:
[![CI](https://github.com/tammoippen/tagic/actions/workflows/CI.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/tammoippen/tagic/actions/workflows/CI.yml) [![PyPi version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/tagic.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/tagic) [![Python Versions](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.11-brightgreen.svg)](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.11-brightgreen.svg) [![Downloads](https://static.pepy.tech/badge/tagic/month)](https://pepy.tech/project/tagic) [![codestyle black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black) [![Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/astral-sh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) # tagic Build html / xhtml with a nice syntax. ## Goals - generate html / xhtml with a nice syntax - have typing support - have editor support for arguments, I used MDN as a reference. - KISS: no more than generation ## Install ```sh > pip install tagic ``` ## Example ```py from tagic.html import * print( html[ head[ title["Example Website"], meta( name="description", content="This is an example website build with tagic", ), ], body[ header(id="header")[h1["Awesome"]], main[p["Some text ", span["with tags"], "in between"]], footer(hidden=True), ], ].render(indent=True) ) ``` Will return ```html Example Website

Awesome

Some text with tags in between

``` ## Similar Projects - [dominate](https://github.com/Knio/dominate): missing the typing support and editor support for arguments - [domonic](https://github.com/byteface/domonic): to broad of a scope, with parsing, js and style and queries. - [domini](https://gitlab.com/deepadmax/domini): missing editor support for arguments - [htmler](https://github.com/ashep/htmler): missing the typing support and editor support for arguments - [PyHTML](https://github.com/cenkalti/pyhtml): missing the typing support and editor support for arguments - [pyhtmlgen](https://github.com/danvran/pyhtmlgen): incomplete - [html](https://pypi.org/project/html/): i do not like syntax and missing the typing support and editor support for arguments - [MarkupPy](https://github.com/tylerbakke/MarkupPy) - [yattag](https://www.yattag.org/): i do not like syntax - [py-microhtml](https://github.com/elonen/py_microhtml): funny tag names and no editor support for arguments - [py3html](https://github.com/ozcanyarimdunya/py3html): no editor support for arguments - [fast-html](https://github.com/pcarbonn/fast_html) - [py2html](https://github.com/am230/py2html) not in pypi, but i like the syntax and took inspiration

classes.name: tagic.base._Meta
documentation: Allow []-access on the class of Nodes.

methods.name: tagic.base.Node.__getitem__
documentation: Add children to the node via []-syntax.

classes.name: tagic.base.NoEscape
documentation: Add string content, that should not be html escaped.

methods.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.add_class
documentation:
Add classes to the 'class' attribute of the tag.

Also makes the classes unique while preserving the order.

(Convenience method.)

methods.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.remove_class
documentation:
Remove classes to the 'class' attribute of the tag.

Also makes the classes unique while preserving the order.

(Convenience method.)

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.accesskey
documentation:
The accesskey global attribute provides a hint for
generating a keyboard shortcut for the current element.
The attribute value must consist of a single printable
character (which includes accented and other characters
that can be generated by the keyboard).

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.autocapitalize
documentation:
The autocapitalize global attribute is an enumerated
attribute that controls whether and how text input is
automatically capitalized as it is entered/edited by the
user.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.autofocus
documentation:
The autofocus global attribute is a Boolean attribute
indicating that an element should be focused on page load,
or when the <dialog> that it is part of is displayed.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.class_
documentation:
The class global attribute is a space-separated list of the
case-sensitive classes of the element. Classes allow CSS
and JavaScript to select and access specific elements via
the class selectors or functions like the DOM method
document.getElementsByClassName.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.contenteditable
documentation:
The contenteditable global attribute is an enumerated
attribute indicating if the element should be editable by
the user. If so, the browser modifies its widget to allow
editing.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.dir
documentation:
The dir global attribute is an enumerated attribute that
indicates the directionality of the element's text.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.draggable
documentation:
The draggable global attribute is an enumerated attribute
that indicates whether the element can be dragged, either
with native browser behavior or the HTML Drag and Drop API.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.enterkeyhint
documentation:
The enterkeyhint global attribute is an enumerated
attribute defining what action label (or icon) to present
for the enter key on virtual keyboards.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.exportparts
documentation:
The exportparts global attribute allows you to select and
style elements existing in nested shadow trees, by
exporting their part names.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hidden
documentation:
The hidden global attribute is an enumerated attribute
indicating that the browser should not render the contents
of the element. For example, it can be used to hide
elements of the page that can't be used until the login
process has been completed.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.id
documentation:
The id global attribute defines an identifier (ID) which
must be unique in the whole document. Its purpose is to
identify the element when linking (using a fragment
identifier), scripting, or styling (with CSS).

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.inert
documentation:
The inert global attribute is a Boolean attribute
indicating that the browser will ignore the element. With
the inert attribute, all of the element's flat tree
descendants (such as modal <dialog>s) that don't otherwise
escape inertness are ignored. The inert attribute also
makes the browser ignore input events sent by the user,
including focus-related events and events from assistive
technologies.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.inputmode
documentation:
The inputmode global attribute is an enumerated attribute
that hints at the type of data that might be entered by the
user while editing the element or its contents. This allows
a browser to display an appropriate virtual keyboard.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.is_
documentation:
The is global attribute allows you to specify that a
standard HTML element should behave like a defined custom
built-in element (see Using custom elements for more details).

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.itemid
documentation:
The itemid global attribute provides microdata in the form
of a unique, global identifier of an item.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.itemprop
documentation: The itemprop global attribute is used to add properties to an item. Every HTML element can have an itemprop attribute specified, and an itemprop consists of a name-value pair. Each name-value pair is called a property, and a group of one or more properties forms an item. Property values are either a string or a URL and can be associated with a very wide range of elements including

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.itemref
documentation:
Properties that are not descendants of an element with the
itemscope attribute can be associated with an item using
the global attribute itemref.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.itemscope
documentation:
itemscope is a boolean global attribute that defines the
scope of associated metadata. Specifying the itemscope
attribute for an element creates a new item, which results
in a number of name-value pairs that are associated with
the element.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.itemtype
documentation:
The global attribute itemtype specifies the URL of the
vocabulary that will be used to define itemprop's (item
properties) in the data structure.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.lang
documentation:
The lang global attribute helps define the language of an
element: the language that non-editable elements are
written in, or the language that the editable elements
should be written in by the user. The attribute contains a
single "language tag" in the format defined in RFC 5646:
Tags for Identifying Languages (also known as BCP 47).

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.nonce
documentation:
The nonce global attribute is a content attribute defining
a cryptographic nonce ("number used once") which can be
used by Content Security Policy to determine whether or not
a given fetch will be allowed to proceed for a given element.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.part
documentation:
The part global attribute contains a space-separated list
of the part names of the element. Part names allows CSS to
select and style specific elements in a shadow tree via the
::part pseudo-element.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.popover
documentation:
The popover global attribute is used to designate an
element as a popover element.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.slot
documentation:
The slot global attribute assigns a slot in a shadow DOM
shadow tree to an element: An element with a slot attribute
is assigned to the slot created by the <slot> element whose
name attribute's value matches that slot attribute's value.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.spellcheck
documentation:
The spellcheck global attribute is an enumerated attribute
that defines whether the element may be checked for
spelling errors.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.style
documentation:
The style global attribute contains CSS styling
declarations to be applied to the element. Note that it is
recommended for styles to be defined in a separate file or
files. This attribute and the <style> element have mainly
the purpose of allowing for quick styling, for example for
testing purposes.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.tabindex
documentation:
The tabindex global attribute allows developers to make
HTML elements focusable, allow or prevent them from being
sequentially focusable (usually with the Tab key, hence the
name) and determine their relative ordering for sequential
focus navigation.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.title
documentation:
The title global attribute contains text representing
advisory information related to the element it belongs to.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.translate
documentation:
The translate global attribute is an enumerated attribute
that is used to specify whether an element's translatable
attribute values and its Text node children should be
translated when the page is localized, or whether to leave
them unchanged.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.virtualkeyboardpolicy
documentation:
The virtualkeyboardpolicy global attribute is an enumerated
attribute. When specified on an element that also uses the
contenteditable attribute, it controls the on-screen
virtual keyboard behavior on devices such as tablets,
mobile phones, or other devices where a hardware keyboard
may not be available.

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.data_attr
documentation:
The data-* global attributes form a class of attributes
called custom data attributes, that allow proprietary
information to be exchanged between the HTML and its DOM
representation by scripts.
Add without 'data-' infront

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.aria_attr
documentation:
The aria-* global attributes.

see https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes

Add without 'aria-' infront

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_boost
documentation:
add or remove progressive enhancement for links and forms

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-boost/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_get
documentation:
issues a GET to the specified URL

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-get/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_post
documentation:
issues a POST to the specified URL

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-post/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_on
documentation:
handle any event with a script inline

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-on/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_push_url
documentation:
pushes the URL into the browser location bar, creating a new history entry

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-push-url/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_select
documentation:
select content to swap in from a response

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-select/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_select_oob
documentation:
select content to swap in from a response, out of band (somewhere ot

   see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-select-oob/
her
than the target)

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_swap
documentation:
controls how content is swapped in (outerHTML, beforeend, afterend, …)

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-swap/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_swap_oob
documentation:
marks content in a response to be out of band (should swap in somewhere other
than the target)

   see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-swap-oob/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_target
documentation:
specifies the target element to be swapped

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-target/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_trigger
documentation:
specifies the event that triggers the request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-trigger/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_vals
documentation:
adds values to the parameters to submit with the request (JSON-formatted)

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-vals/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_confirm
documentation:
shows a confirm() dialog before issuing a request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-confirm/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_delete
documentation:
issues a DELETE to the specified URL

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-delete/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_disable
documentation:
disables htmx processing for the given node and any children nodes

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-disable/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_disinherit
documentation:
control and disable automatic attribute inheritance for child nodes

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-disinherit/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_encoding
documentation:
changes the request encoding type

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-encoding/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_ext
documentation:
extensions to use for this element

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-ext/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_headers
documentation:
adds to the headers that will be submitted with the request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-headers/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_history
documentation:
prevent sensitive data being saved to the history cache

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-history/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_history_elt
documentation:
the element to snapshot and restore during history navigation

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-history-elt/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_include
documentation:
include additional data in requests

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-include/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_indicator
documentation:
the element to put the htmx-request class on during the request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-indicator/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_params
documentation:
filters the parameters that will be submitted with a request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-params/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_patch
documentation:
issues a PATCH to the specified URL

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-patch/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_preserve
documentation:
specifies elements to keep unchanged between requests

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-preserve/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_prompt
documentation:
shows a prompt() before submitting a request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-prompt/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_put
documentation:
issues a PUT to the specified URL

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-put/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_replace_url
documentation:
replace the URL in the browser location bar

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-replace_url/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_request
documentation:
configures various aspects of the request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-request/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_sse
documentation:
has been moved to an extension. Documentation for older versions

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-sse/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_sync
documentation:
control how requests made by different elements are synchronized

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-sync/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_validate
documentation:
force elements to validate themselves before a request

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-validate/

class_variables.name: tagic.html.HTMLElement.hx_ws
documentation:
has been moved to an extension. Documentation for older versions

see https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-ws/
